mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-09-01 reflects emergency easing from March 2020 shock
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which translates into increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8% on 2020-09-01 reflects recovery from April 2020 peak of 14.7%
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between lagged UNRATE changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 6-month moving average while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated, the joint signal indicates robust consumer and residential demand that sustains corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 6-month MA and HOUST > 1.2M units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 537.5B and HOUST at 1.416M on 2020-08-01 show post-pandemic demand rebound
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with housing stimulus
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals by > 10% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 2% month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and demand higher risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.36 on 2020-08-01 reflects partial recovery from April 2020 trough of 84.5
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistically significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, the real interest rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that benefits equity valuations through lower real discount rates and inflation-hedging demand, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.21 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% on 2020-09-01 indicate negative real rates
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Joint signal (negative real rate) fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains above 6.5%, it signals labor market slack that constrains wage inflation and supports equity valuations by reducing corporate cost pressures, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141.72M and UNRATE at 7.8% on 2020-09-01 show slack labor market
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with slack
failure_condition: The interaction signal (slack labor market) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or fails to outperform single-series UNRATE signal by > 5% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates from prior quarter by more than 1 percentage point annualized, it signals weakening economic momentum that triggers equity repricing downward as investors reduce earnings growth expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth declines > 1pp from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17302.51B on 2020-04-01 reflects Q2 2020 contraction from COVID-19 shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the lagged GDP deceleration signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 12-month moving average while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year, the joint signal indicates supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations through margin expansion and real earnings growth, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.36 and CPIAUCSL at 260.21 on 2020-08-01 show supply recovery with contained inflation
failure_condition: The interaction signal (supply growth without inflation) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal underperforms single INDPRO signal by > 10% in Sharpe ratio.
